operation: First, a workplane is created on the XZ plane, where an 8-unit by 4-unit rectangle is drawn and extruded 6 units along the Y axis to form the base rectangular prism. Next, the face at the maximum Y position of the base is selected to establish a new workplane, with the sketch origin shifted by -2 units in the X direction and 0 units in the Z direction. A closed 2D profile is then sketched: starting at the offset origin, a straight line is drawn to coordinate (2, 3), followed by a sagitta arc with a -1.5 sagitta parameter ending at coordinate (6, 0), before closing the path back to the origin. This profile is extruded 2 units outward along the Y axis, combined with the existing base block to produce the final solid part.
geometry: The model is a single monolithic solid consisting of a primary rectangular prism block and an irregular protruding feature attached to one of its end faces. The protruding feature has a straight sloped upper edge, a concave curved lower edge, and flat side and end faces, with no cutouts, holes, or internal cavities present across the entire part.
function: This is a custom single-piece positioning or mounting component, with a main rectangular base for stable attachment and a shaped protruding tab designed to interface with a matching curved mating feature in a larger assembly.
